@chowcollection @phathodl So Bitcoin is revolutionary because it’s both a payments system and a monetary system. It’s these two things, both revolutionary and both combined together for the first time in one network.

@chowcollection @phathodl , meaning we inflate more and more over time. So this second characteristic, its monetary traits, makes it so that this token will be used more and more as money because people will trust it more.

@chowcollection @phathodl , meaning its inflation reduces over time until it reaches a hard cap, the famous hard cap of 21 million Bitcoin. Today we’re at 18.5 million. So it’s the opposite of modern money, that we can fiat money, where the issuance is unpredictable and inflationary

@chowcollection @phathodl Why? Because it has every characteristic of good money that has been used for millennia. This is fungibility, divisibility, transportable, resistant to attack, costly to produce, & in the case of Bitcoin it’s is predictable and disinflationary

@chowcollection @phathodl The second reason why it’s revolutionary is that the way the network is secured includes the issuance of new tokens, both digital and auditable, that we call bitcoin (the asset) that can be used as money. I’m not saying it’s money but it’s usable like money.

@chowcollection @phathodl , meaning trustless. And it’s done in a super secure, censorship-resistant & programmable way. So this is the first reason why it’s totally revolutionary - this didn’t exist before & now it’s possible, thus opening many possibilities in different areas - notably for payrolls.

@chowcollection @phathodl Bitcoin is revolutionary for 2 reasons.

👊 Pack it with punch

Copywriting for SMS is tricky to master for 3 reasons: 

1) It requires a style specific to the medium 📱

2) SMS is a long-term play - you need enough content to sustain it🗓

3) 65% of brands don’t have an SMS strategy in place so lack in-house SMS copywriting expertise 🤓

Hey Alexa, what is conversational commerce? 🤷‍♀️

Conversational commerce is the overall transformation of the online store into conversational channels like SMS and WhatsApp 👀

Further resources: 

How to start your SMS marketing from scratch https://t.co/JlZuon9SSq

7️⃣ Nail your content

SMS is a long-term play requiring tailor-made content

It's no good having a week's worth of material then running out of things to say 👀

You also can't just plonk your email newsletter copy into an SMS either - create it specifically for the channel ✍️

6️⃣ Be ready to speak 1:1

The recipients of your messages are real humans 🙋‍♀️

They've also got a phone and a network plan so can message you back in seconds

Be prepared with a credible chatbot, or god forbid - a human of your own - to get back to them 💬

5️⃣ Offer value 

Don’t spam customers with product-heavy messaging - people will switch off 💀

Lead with personalised, value-filled content to build engagement

4️⃣ Educate your customers 

Because 65% of businesses have no SMS strategy at all, most people have never spoken to a brand via text

They're unlikely to give you their phone number if they don't understand the value ❌

Communicate to consumers what’s in it for them 👩‍🏫

3️⃣ Get complaint

The use of SMS by businesses is heavily regulated 👮‍♂️

Ensure you know the laws applicable to your region

The key legislation you want to look out for are TCPR if you're operating in the US, and GDPR in the EU

2️⃣ Choose an SMS platform 

Not all SMS marketing platforms are equal
